name: 考研英语二·核心难句
desc: 高频考点 8 句，覆盖同位语从句、定语从句嵌套、状语从句、分词短语、it 形式主语、并列结构、否定前置倒装、比较结构。

sentences.en: The argument that mass tourism will inevitably lead to cultural homogenization is one that economists have repeatedly challenged.
sentences.cn: 认为大众旅游必然导致文化同质化的观点，是经济学家一再挑战的论断。

parts.text: that mass tourism will inevitably lead to cultural homogenization
parts.note: 同位语从句，解释 argument 的内容

sentences.tip: 主干是 The argument is one。两个 that 角色不同：第一个引导**同位语从句**解释 argument 的内容（that 后是完整句子）；第二个引导**定语从句**修饰 one（that 后缺宾语，challenged 后无宾语）。
sentences.trap: 看到 that 别一律当定语从句。判断方法：that 后是完整句（主谓宾全）→同位语从句；that 后缺主/宾→定语从句。

sentences.en: Companies which adopt strategies that prioritize long-term value over short-term gains often outperform their competitors in markets where consumer trust matters most.
sentences.cn: 在消费者信任最为重要的市场中，那些采用优先长期价值而非短期收益策略的公司，往往跑赢竞争对手。

parts.text: that prioritize long-term value over short-term gains
parts.note: 定语从句2，修饰 strategies（嵌套在从句1内）

sentences.tip: 三层从句嵌套要逐层剥：① Companies often outperform competitors in markets（主干）；② markets 被 where 从句修饰；③ Companies 被 which 从句修饰；④ strategies 被 that 从句修饰（这层在 which 从句内部）。
sentences.trap: 嵌套从句别从前往后线性翻译。先抓主干 SVO，从句作为附加信息后置或前置整合。

sentences.en: Driven by rising labor costs and stagnant productivity, many manufacturers have begun to relocate production overseas, a move that has reshaped global supply chains.
sentences.cn: 受劳动力成本上升和生产率停滞所迫，许多制造商已开始将生产转移到海外，这一举措重塑了全球供应链。

parts.text: Driven by rising labor costs and stagnant productivity
parts.note: 过去分词短语作原因状语，逻辑主语=manufacturers
同位语，复指 relocate production overseas 整个动作

parts.text: that has reshaped global supply chains
parts.note: 定语从句，修饰 a move

sentences.tip: 句首 Driven by... 是过去分词短语作状语（被动含义），逻辑主语必须和主句主语一致——这里是 manufacturers 被 costs and productivity 驱动。结尾 a move 是名词性同位语，整体复指前面整个事件。
sentences.trap: 考研英语二常考分词短语逻辑主语错位（dangling modifier）。判断分词短语作状语时，先确认主句主语能不能『承担』这个动作。

sentences.en: It remains unclear whether the policies introduced last year have produced the economic benefits that supporters claimed they would deliver.
sentences.cn: 去年推行的那些政策，是否带来了支持者声称会带来的经济利益，目前仍不清楚。

parts.note: 真正主语：whether 引导的主语从句
过去分词短语作后置定语，修饰 policies

parts.text: that supporters claimed they would deliver
parts.note: 定语从句修饰 benefits（含省略 that 的宾语从句）

sentences.tip: 结构是 It remains unclear + whether 主语从句。主语从句内部还有定语从句套宾语从句：that supporters claimed [(that) they would deliver]。整体逻辑：『不清楚 + 政策是否带来了 + 支持者声称会带来的好处』。
sentences.trap: 过去分词作后置定语很容易和主句谓语混淆——这里 introduced 是分词修饰 policies，真正的谓语是 have produced。

sentences.en: Some economists view the reform as a necessary step toward sustainable growth, while others argue that it imposes costs disproportionately on small businesses.
sentences.cn: 一些经济学家把这项改革视为通往可持续增长的必要一步，而另一些则认为它给小企业带来了不成比例的成本负担。

parts.text: as a necessary step toward sustainable growth
parts.note: as 引导宾补，view A as B 结构

parts.text: that it imposes costs disproportionately on small businesses

sentences.tip: while 在这里是**对比连词**（不是『当…时候』），连接两个对比观点。view A as B 是固定搭配——『把 A 视为 B』。others 后省略了 economists。
sentences.trap: while 有两义：① 时间『当…时』；② 对比『而』。考研英语二多考第二种，看上下文是不是在做观点对比。

sentences.en: It is not the technology itself but the way it is integrated into daily workflows that determines whether digital transformation succeeds or fails.
sentences.cn: 决定数字化转型成败的，不是技术本身，而是技术被整合进日常工作流程的方式。

parts.text: not the technology itself but the way
parts.note: 被强调的部分（真正的主语）

parts.text: it is integrated into daily workflows
parts.note: 定语从句修饰 way（关系词 in which 省略）

parts.text: whether digital transformation succeeds or fails

sentences.tip: It is...that... 强调结构，被强调的是『不是技术本身而是整合方式』(not A but B 结构)。去掉 It is 和 that，剩下的句子完整：『not the technology itself but the way (in which) it is integrated into daily workflows determines whether...』。
sentences.trap: It is...that... 既可能是强调句，也可能是 it 形式主语 + that 主语从句。区分：去掉 It is 和 that 后能不能拼回完整句——能=强调句；不能=形式主语。

sentences.en: Not until governments recognized the full scale of the problem did they begin to invest seriously in renewable energy.
sentences.cn: 直到政府认识到问题的全部严重性，他们才开始认真投资可再生能源。

parts.text: Not until governments recognized the full scale of the problem
parts.note: 否定时间状语前置（含 until 引导的时间状语从句）
助动词提到主语前——部分倒装的标志

parts.text: begin to invest seriously in renewable energy
parts.note: 主句谓语（倒装后动词原形回到主语后）

sentences.tip: 否定词/否定短语（Not until, Never, Hardly, Only then…）置于句首时，主句要用『部分倒装』：把助动词/be 提到主语前。还原正常语序：They did not begin to invest seriously… until governments recognized…
sentences.trap: 倒装的是**主句**，不是 until 引导的从句。从句 governments recognized… 语序不变；只有主句 they begin → did they begin 发生倒装。

sentences.en: The students who spend their time actively solving problems tend to retain knowledge far longer than those who merely reread their notes.
sentences.cn: 那些把时间花在主动解题上的学生，往往比仅仅重读笔记的学生记得更久。

parts.text: who spend their time actively solving problems
far longer than those who merely reread their notes
parts.note: 比较状语（than + those + 定语从句 who…）

sentences.tip: 主干其实很短：The students tend to retain knowledge longer。两个 who 从句分别修饰 students 和 those；far 修饰比较级 longer，表示『久得多』。
sentences.trap: 别把定语从句的谓语 spend 当成主句谓语——主句真正的谓语是 tend to retain。找到主语后第一个『不在从句里』的动词才是主句谓语。
